items.title: Law, order and $1b surplus form pillars of Labor's upcoming budget

children.content: The government has pledged to invest heavily in a crime crackdown and deliver a surplus in its upcoming 2026/27 state budget.

items.title: Another Melbourne nightclub targeted in 'terrifying' black market alcohol wars

children.content: Ladida nightclub is the latest licensed venue to be targeted by arsonists in Melbourne, as industry representatives fear an escalation of black market alcohol wars.

image.altText: A sign saying Ladida.

items.title: High Court to consider who owns rights to abattoir secret camera footage

children.content: A goat abattoir, a group of animal rights activists and the copyright act will be the focus today in a High Court challenge over who owns the rights to footage captured by secret cameras.

image.altText: The exterior of a modern concrete building with a sign out front reading "High Court of Australia".

items.title: Accused underworld shooters far from trained assassins, defence argues

children.content: Lawyers for two men accused of murdering underworld figure Gavin Preston tell a jury their clients did not have the skills required to carry out a well-planned execution.

gallery.altText: Crime figure Gavin Preston looks unsmilingly into the camera as he poses for a photo.

thumbnail.altText: Crime figure Gavin Preston looks unsmilingly into the camera as he poses for a photo.

avatar.alt: A man with dark skin in a suit looks at the camera

items.title: Patients in need of home pharmacy help missing out due to caps

children.content: Pharmacists say government funding caps limiting the number of patients they can see in their homes for medication reviews means many are missing out on life-changing help.

image.altText: Two women sitting together looking and reading medication information

items.title: Who pays to put food on the table during a global crisis?

children.content: As the price of food and agricultural production goes up during the Iran conflict, supermarkets are being urged to absorb more of the cost.

image.altText: A supermarket fresh food aisle

items.title: Brisbane Lions player under investigation for alleged homophobic slur in VFL

children.content: The AFL faces another homophobic slur case, with a Brisbane Lions player alleged to have used a slur in a VFL game against Coburg.

gallery.altText: A headshot of a player taking part in the AFL Draft Combine in 2025 with an AFL Draft logo behind him.

thumbnail.altText: A headshot of a player taking part in the AFL Draft Combine in 2025 with an AFL Draft logo behind him.

items.title: Worker shortage sees people struggle in 'most extreme form of homelessness'

children.content: Victoria needs almost five times as many outreach field workers to support people who are sleeping rough, according to a study by the Council to Homeless Persons.

gallery.altText: A person kneeling on a footpath under a blanket with a sign saying 'homeless please help'

thumbnail.altText: A person kneeling on a footpath under a blanket with a sign saying 'homeless please help'

items.title: Alleged Anzac Day hecklers face court over 2025 booing

children.content: Witnesses have told a court they were "disgusted" when a group of men interrupted last year's Anzac Day Dawn Service in Melbourne.

image.altText: ANZAC Day dawn service at Shrine of Remembrance

items.title: Melbourne bar rammed, doused with petrol amid suspicious fires

children.content: Police are investigating multiple suspicious fires including an attack on Electric Bar in Prahran, a suspicious house fire in Caulfield North and a fire at a Tullamarine kebab shop.

image.altText: A burnt out ca.

items.title: Victorian teachers not to strike, council workers proceed with 'historic' action

children.content: The Australian Education Union has suspended part of its industrial campaign during budget week, but Australia's 'largest-ever' strike by council workers will still take place.

image.altText: A man with dark hair and a beard in a red shirt holds up a sign that says "Lowest funded schools lowest funded teachers".

items.title: Court hears allegations Nicky Winmar 'bashed' woman's head against door

children.content: A Victorian court has heard allegations former AFL great Nicky Winmar "bashed" a woman's head against a wooden door with such force it cracked during an alleged assault in May 2025.

image.altText: Two men walk into court

items.title: Sister of deceased rock climber Angela Ha says the best way to honour her is to 'live life to the fullest'

children.content: Michelle Ha's older sister Angela died after falling from the Organ Pipes while rock climbing at Arapiles in western Victoria last week.

gallery.altText: Woman closed mouth smiling in a selfie on top of a mountain
One sister smiling, second sister with tongue out and hoding first sister, behind birthday cake at wooden dinner table

items.title: Island residents fight land valuations as authorities block building work

children.content: Residents on Phillip Island, where building is limited because of inundation and erosion risk, are battling to have their property valuations reduced.

image.altText: A group of older people, dressed for cold weather, stand in front of a street sign. One lady is holding a little dog.

items.title: Victoria's housing plan will homogenise suburbs, campaigners say

children.content: Residents' groups across Victoria have formed an alliance ahead of the state election, calling for the community and councils to be given a voice when it comes to approving high-density housing.

image.altText: Three people look at the camera standing on an urban street.

items.title: By-election of no consequence leaves some major party insiders disturbed

children.content: The Nepean by-election proves Victoria's election in November is likely to be tough for the two major parties, Richard Willingham writes.

articles.title: Liberals focus on 'lessons' for Victorian election after decisive Nepean win

children.content: It took less than three hours from the start of vote counting for election analysts to call the result in the Liberals' favour, with a win for former local mayor Anthony Marsh.

image.altText: A man with light red hair and glasses in a suit stands on a stage with his hands clasped while a blonde woman speaks into a mic.

gallery.altText: A large group of people stand close together in a room with one arm each raised in the air as they cheer.

thumbnail.altText: A large group of people stand close together in a room with one arm each raised in the air as they cheer.

avatar.alt: Richard Willingham in front of a white background, stares straight down the barrel.

items.title: A hacker built a tool to track police officers. Then he tried to warn them

children.content: A security flaw means the location of devices used by police, potentially including undercover officers and those travelling home, is visible to anyone with a phone or laptop.

image.altText: A man wearing a dark hoodie is silhouetted against the lights of a police station with police cars in front.

items.title: Council struggling with erosion wants big polluters to pay to fix it

children.content: Councillors in a Victorian shire battling coastal erosion say fossil fuel giants should help pay for damage caused by climate-related environmental disasters.

image.altText: An aerial photo of a beach with sandbags and a lifesaving clubhouse behind

items.title: A region promised a hospital by 2028 watches as cows graze on the site

children.content: Victoria's fast-growing West Gippsland region was promised a new hospital by 2028, but work has not started.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a hospital nestled in green space.

items.title: Surgeons thought about taking Travis's face off before they saved him with a 'crazy idea'

children.content: Travis Reyes survived a horror military aircraft crash but what came after was a two-year battle from doctors across Australia and the US to save his life.

image.altText: A man in a green shirt with scars on the right side of his face and glasses.

items.title: What this truck full of fibreglass sheets tells us about building price pressures

children.content: On Australia's busiest freight route, it takes four shots of coffee, $2,553 of fuel and a little hair metal to keep the wheels of the economy turning.

items.title: Murray-Darling Basin's apex predator back from the brink

children.content: Murray cod numbers have bounced back across much of Victoria and NSW, and that's good news for the whole Murray-Darling Basin ecosystem.

image.altText: Close up of a Murray cod.

items.title: Love story: When life changed in an instant, Lucy was Beau's rock

children.content: The goalposts moved dramatically on Beau and Lucy Vernon's life plans, but it brought them closer together.

image.altText: A woman in a white wedding dress standing next to a man using a mobility aid

items.title: Making wine in memory of officers killed by Dezi Freeman

children.content: The Porepunkah community and Victoria Police pick grapes together to make wine in honour of Neal Thompson and Vadim De Waart-Hottart, who were killed by Dezi Freeman.

image.altText: A police officer pour grapes into large container on a vineyard

items.title: 'If the cops came, you'd slip out the back': The shifting role of Aussie pubs

children.content: From a tiny Riverina pub to a polished inner-city institution, Australia's local pubs are changing hands at record pace. As big money pours in, what happens to the communities that built them?

image.altText: Graphic illustration of arms and hands holding schooners of beer 'cheersing' under old wrought iron balcony decoration

items.title: Beck spent decades as an alt-rock anomaly. Now he's going symphonic

children.content: The alt-rock anomaly has toured Australia numerous times over the past 30 years. But this month is his first accompanied by symphonic orchestras.

image.altText: Birds eye photo of man with curly hair in corduroy suit sitting on piano stool leaning back on grand piano

items.title: Brisbane Lions coach slams 'hero' parent after whiteboard leak

children.content: Chris Fagan has expressed his disappointment after the leaking of confidential Brisbane Lions notes on Essendon players.

image.altText: An image of whiteboard appeaqringg to show Lions' analysis of Bombers players.

items.title: Police pulled him over on his farm. He was tasered 30 seconds later

children.content: Disturbing cases of people wrongly tasered by police on their farm and at school has raised questions over whether officers are too reliant on the devices.

gallery.altText: A composite image of  a man being threatened with a taser then being tasered by police at night.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of  a man being threatened with a taser then being tasered by police at night.

items.title: A-League finals result in mixed bag for World Cup bolters

children.content: Socceroos hopeful Marcus Younis sees his season come to an end but a host of Australian young guns progress after an enthralling pair of A-League finals.

image.altText: Two soccer players argue with each other.

items.title: A 17-year-old Go-Betweens song inspired Robert Forster's debut novel

children.content: The Go-Betweens frontman spent years developing the story, which follows a pair of musicians on the run from the law as they try to clear their names.

items.title: Man shot at and venues set on fire in latest Melbourne attacks

children.content: A man has been shot at during an aggravated home invasion, and a pub and restaurant were set on fire overnight in Melbourne.

image.altText: Police tape at a crime scene

items.title: Man airlifted with life-threatening injuries after caravan park brawl

children.content: A man has been airlifted to hospital with life-threatening injuries after being stabbed during a wild brawl at a caravan park in a small Victorian town.

image.altText: Four police officers in high-vis uniforms, with their backs to the camera.

items.title: 1976 in music: The big albums turning 50 this year

children.content: The music industry is always in a state of flux, but what was happening in 1976 was akin to the dinosaurs looking up and seeing a meteor on the way.

items.title: Second of two new Spirit of Tasmania ferries arrives in Geelong

children.content: Following a seven-week journey from Scotland, the Spirit of Tasmania V has arrived in Geelong. It will undergo gantry testing tomorrow before being temporarily relocated to Station Pier in Melbourne.

image.altText: A large red and white ferry with SPIRIT OF TASMANIA on it sailing through a bay.

items.title: The four women who left 'Australia Street' to try and leave Syria

children.content: Four Australian women, their children and grandchildren are stuck in limbo in Syria after leaving a refugee camp for family members of suspected Islamic State fighters. This is what we know about them.

image.altText: A group of women wearing hijabs and face masks, accompanied by a group of children.
